inline_comments: Slack-hosted files typically come from *.slack.com and redirect to Slack CDN domains.
Include a small allowlist of known Slack domains to avoid leaking tokens if a file URL
is ever spoofed or mishandled.

Fetches a URL with Authorization header, handling cross-origin redirects. Node.js fetch strips Authorization headers on cross-origin redirects for security. Slack's file URLs redirect to CDN domains with pre-signed URLs that don't need the Authorization header, so we handle the initial auth request manually.
Initial request with auth and manual redirect handling
If not a redirect, return the response directly
Handle redirect - the redirected URL should be pre-signed and not need auth
Resolve relative URLs against the original
Only follow safe protocols (we do NOT include Authorization on redirects).
Follow the redirect without the Authorization header
(Slack's CDN URLs are pre-signed and don't need it)

Slack voice messages (audio clips, huddle recordings) carry a `subtype` of `"slack_audio"` but are served with a `video/*` MIME type (e.g. `video/mp4`, `video/webm`).  Override the primary type to `audio/` so the media-understanding pipeline routes them to transcription.
Narrow this parser to Slack's explicit "shared/forwarded" attachment payloads.
Downloads all files attached to a Slack message and returns them as an array. Returns `null` when no files could be downloaded.
Note: fetchRemoteMedia calls fetchImpl(url) with the URL string today and
handles size limits internally. Provide a fetcher that uses auth once, then lets
the redirect chain continue without credentials.
Guard against auth/login HTML pages returned instead of binary media.
Allow user-provided HTML files through.

Fetches the most recent messages in a Slack thread (excluding the current message). Used to populate thread context when a new thread session starts. Uses cursor pagination and keeps only the latest N retained messages so long threads still produce up-to-date context without unbounded memory growth.
Slack recommends no more than 200 per page.
Keep messages with text OR file attachments
For file-only messages, create a placeholder showing attached filenames
